violations.text: Establishment observed to be using Time as a Public Health Control for Rice at the Sushi Bar. A request for approval with written procedures to use time as a public health control for rice is to be submitted to RIDOH CFP Supervisor. (Contact information provided at time of inspection.) Once submitted and approved, the procedures, approval letter, and records are to be maintained in the FOOD ESTABLISHMENT and made available to the REGULATORY AUTHORITY upon request. New Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2025

Raw animal foods (such as raw fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables) in a refrigeration unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Ice machine was taken out of service and bags of ice were used. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment is to utilize three compartment sink to wash, rinse, and sanitize equipment until dish machine is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024
